mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and lower equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cut periods.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, causing investors to price in lower corporate earnings and driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses predictive power when unemployment rises during early-cycle recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of rising retail sales and stable consumer prices indicates robust consumer demand without inflationary pressure, supporting corporate revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds 5% annually while inflation simultaneously accelerates above 3%.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase reflects rapid capacity utilization and supply chain normalization, triggering short-term momentum buying and positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The signal generates negative forward returns during supply-constrained regimes where industrial output rises but input bottlenecks suppress profit margins.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly growth in real gross domestic product confirms expanding aggregate demand and corporate profit foundations, systematically driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns turn negative in > 50% of quarters where GDPC1 growth exceeds 2% annualized.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly acceleration in consumer prices forces markets to anticipate aggressive central bank tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets price in inflation pass-through successfully, resulting in positive forward returns in > 65% of high-inflation trigger months.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts signals synchronized strength in labor income and residential investment, boosting consumer spending and yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal loses predictive accuracy when payroll growth is concentrated in low-wage sectors while housing construction remains stagnant.

mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate hikes during a late-cycle expansion phase tighten financial conditions and cap earnings growth, systematically producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Rate hikes during late-cycle phases fail to suppress equity returns, with positive forward returns occurring in > 60% of instances due to strong earnings momentum.
